title: A Procedure for Evaluating Sensitivity to Within-Person Change: Can Mood Measures in Diary Studies Detect Change Reliably?
population: Daily diary datasets examining mood change over time
main_findings: Measures built for between-person differences are not automatically reliable for within-person change.
Short diary mood measures can reliably detect within-person changes if evaluated specifically for that purpose.
Within-person emotional tracking needs measurement design that is sensitive to change over time, not only trait differences.
allowed_inferences: Aurum should evaluate mood labels and emotional indicators for sensitivity to change within the same user over time.
Aurum can justify short repeated measures if they are designed for longitudinal sensitivity rather than static classification.
forbidden_inferences: Do not use this paper as evidence that Aurum improves mood.
Do not turn measurement reliability into a user-facing efficacy claim.
prompt_guidance: None. This card is for evaluation and product measurement design.
notes: Important for stats, trend cards, and internal evaluation. This card should inform how Aurum measures change, not what DeepSeek says to users.
